name: Cloud RAN Vendor Platform Health
trigger: Active probe detects increasing call setup failures correlated with a single vDU vendor platform in three regional clusters.

autonomyLevels.L1: Engineering team correlates probe failures with vendor platform logs. Vendor ticket raised.
autonomyLevels.L2: Traffic rerouted away from degraded vendor clusters. Vendor escalated automatically.
autonomyLevels.L3: Automated vendor platform health scoring. SLA breach clock started. Compensation claim prepared.
autonomyLevels.L4: Autonomous traffic management routes around failing vendor components. Vendor notified with full evidence package. Recovery validated before traffic restoration.

economicOutcome: Subscriber impact from third-party vendor failure contained. Vendor SLA claim evidence captured.
economicCalculation: Cloud RAN vendor SLA typically includes credits for availability breach. For a €5M annual contract with 99.99% availability SLA: each hour of breach = €570 credit. Detection speed determines credit capture.
combinationUpgrade: Add Emblasoft nScan (passive probe) to identify which subscribers were affected during the vendor platform degradation — enabling precise impact quantification for the vendor claim.
